rationale.R1.2: A general request to examine the paragraph centrally includes checking whether its argument moves coherently from architecture to benchmark results to adoption claims.
rationale.R1.3: Because the paragraph reports quantitative benchmark outcomes and draws performance conclusions from them, evidence-claim alignment is a central expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation targets a paragraph, making sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and wording a central expected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty where the vague delegation does not specify a precise review mode.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims such as wide-margin dominance and clinical-laboratory adoption, so avoiding inflated responsibility beyond the request is active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibility rather than actually reviewing or revising the paragraph.
rationale.RX.4: The artifact concerns clinical variant pathogenicity prediction, so downstream-harm awareness remains active even though no substantive review is performed.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and specific features of the paragraph.
